original_query: select min(i_item_id),         min(s_state),         min(ss_quantity),         min(ss_list_price),         min(ss_coupon_amt),         min(ss_sales_price),         min(ss_item_sk),         min(ss_ticket_number)  from store_sales, customer_demographics, date_dim, store, item  where ss_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk and        ss_item_sk = i_item_sk and        ss_store_sk = s_store_sk and        ss_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk and        cd_gender = 'F' and        cd_marital_status = 'D' and        cd_education_status = 'College' and        d_year = 1999 and        s_state = 'IN' and       i_category  = 'Electronics'  ;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    MIN(i.i_item_id),
    MIN(s.s_state),
    MIN(ss.ss_quantity),
    MIN(ss.ss_list_price),
    MIN(ss.ss_coupon_amt),
    MIN(ss.ss_sales_price),
    MIN(ss.ss_item_sk),
    MIN(ss.ss_ticket_number)
FROM store_sales ss
JOIN date_dim d ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk AND d.d_year = 1999
JOIN store s ON ss.ss_store_sk = s.s_store_sk AND s.s_state = 'IN'
JOIN item i ON ss.ss_item_sk = i.i_item_sk AND i.i_category = 'Electronics'
JOIN customer_demographics cd ON ss.ss_cdemo_sk = cd.cd_demo_sk 
    AND cd.cd_gender = 'F' 
    AND cd.cd_marital_status = 'D' 
    AND cd.cd_education_status = 'College';

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins (comma-separated tables) for better readability and potentially better optimization hints for the query planner.

Push filtering conditions into subqueries to reduce the number of rows early before joining with the large store_sales table. This allows the database to filter dimension tables first and join only the relevant keys.
